title: Structure of a Nerve
description: OpenStax illustration demonstrating the hierarchical connective tissue organization of a peripheral nerve. It shows individual axons wrapped in endoneurium, bundled into fascicles by the perineurium, and finally encased together with blood vessels by the tough outer epineurium.
clinical_notes: The structural integrity of these layers dictates nerve recovery. Neuropraxia (myelin injury) recovers quickly. Axonotmesis (axon severed, endoneurium intact) recovers at 1mm/day. Neurotmesis (entire nerve severed) requires surgical repair. OTs track recovery timelines using Semmes-Weinstein monofilaments and progressive sensory re-education.
